content:
I just added a "add to Google Calendar" link on the website. See https://github.com/bostonopen/bostonopen.github.io/pull/20

Here's how it looks:
I wonder if an ICS link to subscribe to would be better
Then it can be add once and have all future meetups show up
FOSSRIT.github.io has a calendar with an ICS feed thats generated with Jekyll if thats a workflow that fits how this site is built

Well, at least one person asked for a Google Calendar link and I use it myself so I don't want it go away. :smile:

But sure, if you want to make a pull request to also add an ics file, please go for it!
Can you at least have partial functionality as a Google user with the ICS link?
Wondering for my own purposes, not even for a tech event.

As a Google Calendar user, I find ics files to be a poor user experience. You click the ics file. It shows up in your "downloads" folder. You dig around in Google Calendar for the right place to import the file. You click import, you browse for the ics file. It's really not great.

I imagine it sucks. I'm just curious if it at least works though as a lowest common denominator for everything. (Though, on a phone I'm surprised that the Google Calendar app doesn't intercept it as soon as you download it)

One time ICS downloads aren't a good experience. Instead what I do is copy the link to the ICS and "subscribe" to the url. Then google calendar does the downloading and updating of events for you as they change
I also wouldn't be surprised if it's possible to set up a Google Calendar link to automate setting that up.

Oh, I see what you mean. Subscribing to a calendar. The main problem (eventually) is having too many subscribed calendars in your sidebar. Plus, it sounds like you'd want to maintain a history whereas my solution is focused only on the next event.
We could always just delete old events from the feed/update it instead of adding new events

Let's make sure to talk about this on Sunday. I'm curious about this for my own purposes. I don't know the proper behavior of ICS. I would think that deleting items would imply that the event didn't happen.
https://moralcode.github.io/bostonopen.github.io demo of the "add to calendar" links
